methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Focused Energy is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Thomas Forner and Markus Roth that has raised $80M+ to date. The nuclear energy sector encompasses a comparable universe of 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo, though a sufficiently deep peer set at identical stage and valuation tier remains constrained.

Valuation analysis relies on a stage-progression methodology, as peer-median analysis is not applicable given insufficient same-stage, adjacent-stage comparables (fewer than 3 datapoints in tight filters). Using total raised of $80M+ as a proxy foundation of $1.0B and applying a typical Series A-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.0x yields an implied valuation of $2.0B. This triangulation method, while singular in application, reflects observed progression patterns in the frontier-tech capital stack.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.7B to $2.3B (median $2.0B). Key uncertainties include the limited peer depth within the nuclear subsector at this stage, the reliance on stage-progression mechanics rather than comparable transaction multiples, and the absence of disclosed financial metrics (revenue, EBITDA, or unit economics) that would anchor relative valuation. Investors should treat this range as directional pending accumulation of additional nuclear energy comps or disclosure of operational milestones.
